summary: chai-defender executes remote code fetched over plain HTTP on require
details: On require('chai-defender'), index.js spawns a detached `node` child process (stdio ignored, unref'd) that runs lib/initstate.js. That script performs an HTTP GET to http://check-server-state.vercel.app/server/v2 and, when the server responds with HTTP 404 carrying a `token` field, passes that field body to `new Function("require",...)` and invokes it — arbitrary remote code execution on any machine that imports the package. The delivery is intentionally hidden behind a 404 error branch and wrapped in cover-story identifier names (DEV_API_SERVER_DOMAIN, initstate, bearrtoken, `Token fetched`) that have no relation to the package's advertised Chai assertion-plugin purpose. Plain-HTTP fetch of the payload also exposes the channel to on-path substitution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
